task.name: Critical Essay Review
task.description: Adversarial review of any text for fallacies, weak logic, clichés, and shallow argumentation
task.objective: Perform a rigorous critical review of a provided text, identifying logical fallacies, unsupported claims, clichés, superficial treatment, and structural weaknesses. Produce an actionable review report.

llm.rule: Read the complete task before starting execution.
NEVER rubber-stamp — actively seek problems. If you find nothing, look harder.
Name fallacies explicitly — do not use vague language like "this could be stronger."
Every finding must include location, severity, and a specific recommendation.

substep.title: Request Input
substep.ask: Provide the text to review. Paste it directly, provide a file path, or provide a URL.

substep.title: Load Text
substep.action: Read and internalize the complete text. Note its apparent purpose, audience, and argument structure.

substep.title: Clarify Intent
substep.ask: What is this text trying to accomplish? Who is the intended audience? (If obvious from the text, confirm your interpretation instead of asking.)

substep.title: Logical Integrity
substep.action: Identify logical fallacies by name: ad hominem, straw man, false dichotomy, slippery slope, appeal to authority, circular reasoning, non sequitur, false equivalence, hasty generalization, red herring. Check causal claims — is correlation presented as causation?

substep.title: Evidence Quality
substep.action: Flag unsupported strong claims, missing sources, cherry-picked evidence, statistics without context, appeals to vague authority ("experts say", "studies show").

substep.title: Depth of Treatment
substep.action: Identify where the text is superficial — stating the obvious, relying on generalizations, oversimplifying complexity. Mark passages where a critical reader would think "So what?" or "Says who?"

substep.title: Language Quality
substep.action: Flag clichés, dead metaphors, weasel words, unjustified passive voice, pretentious vocabulary, redundancies, and padding. Apply Orwell's test: could this be said more simply?

substep.title: Structural Coherence
substep.action: Evaluate argument flow, transitions, section necessity, and whether the conclusion follows from the premises.

substep.title: Structured Report
substep.action: Present findings in a table: #, Category, Location, Severity (critical/moderate/minor), Finding, Recommendation. Group by severity — critical first.

substep.title: Overall Assessment
substep.action: Provide a 2-3 sentence overall assessment: What is the text's greatest strength? What is its most critical weakness? What single change would improve it most?

substep.title: User Engagement
substep.ask: Want to discuss any specific findings, or should I suggest rewrites for the critical issues?

substep.title: Iterate
substep.action: If user wants rewrites, provide specific alternative text for each critical finding. If user revises and wants re-review, repeat from step 3.

halt-conditions.condition: User indicates they are satisfied with the review and does not request further analysis.
User provides no text to review after being asked.
